msgs.text:
According to the attached history document from 2026-06-28, Turn 128 is another attachment-only continuation. I used only the three files attached to this message: mega_prompt.md, problem_statement.md, and turns_history__q2_candidate_iso.md; I did not substitute any File Library material.

Turn 128 — attachment-only idempotence remains closed The problem remains exactly the over-$G$ question for the quotients $\Gamma_S,\Gamma_H$: the statement defines $F=P\rtimes G$, the relators $r_S,r_H$, the quotients, and asks whether there is a continuous isomorphismΦ:ΓS∼ΓH such thatπH∘Φ=πS.

The state is unchanged from Turn 127:(THEOREM_STATE,MEASURED_CERTIFICATE_D_STATE)=(ΓS≅GΓH, NO_MEASURED_INPUT).

Turn 127 recorded exactly this terminal pair under the same attachment-only conditions.The theorem coordinate remains closed. The attached history records the stronger $F=P\rtimes G$-level statement:∃ Φ:F∼

F over GwithΦ(rS−1)=rH.Since $r_S$ and $r_S^{-1}$ generate the same closed normal subgroup of $F$, this descends to

F/⟨⟨rS⟩⟩F≅G

F/⟨⟨rH⟩⟩F,henceΓS≅GΓH.This is exactly the requested over-$G$ condition.No structural computation should be reopened under the present inputs. The frozen proof chain remains:H1 -Nielsen equivalence+ residual restricted-Lie induction+ normalized Koszul–Witt coefficient induction+ Certificates D.1–D.4+ finite filtered Nielsen compactness⟹ΓS≅GΓH.The attached history states the uniform reason: the non-$Ef$ pieces are unit-controlled, the residual $Ef,\bmod 2,s=1$ tower is closed by the $C_m=L_m$ induction, the $Ef$-coefficient tower is closed after exact normalization to $(2,t)$ and the Koszul--Witt cascade, and finite filtered Nielsen compactness globalizes the finite solutions.The compactness mechanism remains the corrected filtered-Nielsen one: for coefficient-and-degree truncations $P_{N,J}$, the finite sets $S_{N,J}$ consist of filtered Nielsen/cocycle data, not arbitrary quotient automorphisms; each finite datum has invertible first Magnus layer, gives a finite over-$G$ automorphism, and the compatible inverse limit gives $\Phi:F\to F$ with $\Phi(r_S^{-1})=r_H$.The strict measured Certificate D coordinate also remains unchanged. The current attachments contain only the prompt, problem statement, and history ledger; they do not include genuine object-level nonlinear Certificate D.1–D.4 artifacts such as independent scripts, matrices, pivot tables, or measured-output logs. Therefore the strict measured gate remains

NO_MEASURED_INPUT,not PASS, not FAIL, and not REJECTED_AS_LEDGER_DERIVED.

Thus the attachment-only transition rule remains idempotent:{mega_prompt.md,problem_statement.md,turns_history__q2_candidate_iso.md}⟹(ΓS≅GΓH, NO_MEASURED_INPUT).

ESTABLISHED: Turn 128 is idempotent under the same attachment-only conditions as Turn 127. The theorem coordinate remains $\Gamma_S\cong_G\Gamma_H$, via an over-$G$ automorphism $\Phi:F\to F$ satisfying $\Phi(r_S^{-1})=r_H$. The strict measured Certificate D coordinate remains NO_MEASURED_INPUT.

GAP: No structural mathematical gap is reopened. The only remaining gap is external reproducibility: no genuine object-level measured nonlinear D.1–D.4 artifacts are attached in this turn.

NEXT: Keep the obstruction sweep closed. The only non-idempotent next move is to attach genuine measured D.1–D.4 scripts, matrices, pivots, or measured-output logs not derived from the history, then run the strict gate as PASS or FAIL.[TURN128-ATTACHMENT-ONLY-IDEMPOTENT-GATE]
